sourceMovie.title: Casper
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Casper works as a fantasy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The fantasy and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hocus Pocus
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Casper clicked for you, Hocus Pocus (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Hocus Pocus 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Casper clicked for you, Hocus Pocus 2 (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Casper?
faq.answer: Try Hocus Pocus, Hocus Pocus 2, IF—each shares DNA with Casper while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Casper?
faq.answer: Casper (1995) is best described as Fantasy, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Casper part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Casper Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
